# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.114.0/http/server.ts";

async function loadModule(modulePath: string): Promise<any> {
  if (!modulePath.startsWith("https://")) {
    throw new Error("Module path must be a secure URL.");
  }
  return await import(modulePath);
}

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module") || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");

  if (!modulePath) {
    return new Response("Module path not specified.", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const module = await loadModule(modulePath);
    if (module && typeof module.default === "function") {
      const result = await module.default();
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), { status: 200 });
    } else {
      return new Response("Module does not export a default function.", { status: 400 });
    }
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(`Error loading module: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}

serve(handler);
